---
title: Elata SDK
description: Build browser apps with camera rPPG first; add EEG when you need brain signals; use Web Bluetooth to connect a Muse-compatible headset.
---

Elata SDK is a mixed Rust and TypeScript monorepo for biosignal products.
The published packages focus on helping app developers add supported sensors to
their browser apps with minimal integration work.

This site is organized around supported sensor workflows (what you want to
build), not internal repo structure.

If you are evaluating the SDK or starting a new app, begin with
`@elata-biosciences/create-elata-demo` instead of cloning this repo or starting
from internal demos.

## Who This Site Is For

Use this site if you are:

- evaluating Elata for a browser app
- adding camera-based rPPG (typical first integration) or optional EEG to an existing app
- maintaining this monorepo and need the canonical package and workflow map

The site is intentionally written to lead consumer developers toward the
published package path first, then point maintainers to internal repo workflows
only when they are the right tool.

## What rPPG And EEG Are

- **rPPG** (the usual first integration) stands for remote photoplethysmography.
  It estimates pulse-related changes from camera video, usually from a face
  region, without a wearable sensor. In apps, that usually means reading
  heart-rate-like metrics and diagnostics from a live camera session. Concrete
  examples include deception or bluffing games that react to pulse changes,
  stress and arousal feedback, and biofeedback-oriented health or wellness
  experiences.
- **EEG** (optional, when your product needs brain signals) measures electrical
  activity from the brain using a wearable headset. In apps, that usually means
  streaming headset data, computing features such as band powers, and turning
  those signals into app state. **Web Bluetooth** is how you connect a
  Muse-compatible headset in supported browsers—see the BLE docs when you need
  live headset streaming, not as a parallel “first app” to rPPG.

## How Apps Typically Use Them

| Signal | Typical app pattern |
| --- | --- |
| rPPG | open the camera, run a session on video frames, then use pulse metrics in deception games, biofeedback loops, stress-aware UX, or wellness diagnostics |
| EEG | connect to a headset (often via Web Bluetooth), stream samples, compute features, then adapt a meditation flow, focus trainer, neurofeedback loop, or simulated performance experience |

## Supported Sensors (App Store)

The SDK supports two sensor families in the browser:

- **Camera-based rPPG (modern browsers + camera permissions)** — primary path
  for most evaluations and many products: run rPPG on video frames via
  WebAssembly.
- **Muse-compatible EEG via Web Bluetooth (Chrome/Edge, secure contexts)** —
  optional brain-signal workflow; **BLE connects the headset** to your EEG stack.
  Muse 2, Muse S classic BLE, Muse S Athena protocol v2. Safari and iOS BLE
  are not supported for this workflow.

## From Sensor To Package

| What you integrate | Install | Typical app entrypoint |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Camera-based rPPG | `@elata-biosciences/rppg-web` | `await createRppgSession({ video, ... })` |
| EEG analysis (no live headset) | `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` | `await initEegWasm()` + analysis functions (e.g. `band_powers`) |
| Live Muse headset (Bluetooth enabled) | `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` + `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble` | `new BleTransport(...)` + `connect()` + `start()` |

## Recommended Reading Order

If you want the fastest route to value:

1. Start with [Quickstart](/quickstart).
2. Open [Build A Browser App](/browser-apps): **rPPG first**, **EEG if you choose**, **Web Bluetooth to enable a headset**, then pick **scaffold** or **step-by-step tutorial** (and see how that differs from the shorter **integration overviews**).
3. Use [Package Selection](/package-selection) when you need package-level rationale and anti-patterns.
4. Follow [Build Your First Elata App](/tutorials/first-app), then use *Where To Go Next* on that page: **camera (rPPG)** first, then **optional EEG**, then **Bluetooth for the headset**, each with **new app (scaffold)** vs **existing app (integrate)**.
5. Jump to the package reference pages after you know which integration path you need.

## Choose By App Need

Default to **camera rPPG** when you want biosignals without hardware or when
you are still exploring the SDK. Add **EEG** when the product needs brain-signal
features. Add **Web Bluetooth** (`eeg-web-ble` with `eeg-web`) when you need a
**live Muse-compatible headset** in the browser.

## Start Here

| Goal | Start with | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Map primary rPPG, optional EEG, and headset Bluetooth | [Build A Browser App](/browser-apps) | Scaffold vs tutorial vs overview in recommended order |
| Try the SDK quickly | [Quickstart](/quickstart) | Fastest path to a working demo app (defaults to rPPG) |
| Pick the right package | [Package Selection](/package-selection) | rPPG first, then EEG, then BLE as headset transport |
| Follow end-to-end onboarding tutorials | [Build Your First Elata App](/tutorials/first-app) | Scaffold one template; next steps follow rPPG → EEG → Bluetooth |
| rPPG integration overview (not a full walkthrough) | [rPPG In A Browser App](/guides/rppg-browser) | Starts with `createRppgSession()` and diagnostics |
| EEG integration overview (not a full walkthrough) | [EEG In A Browser App](/guides/eeg-browser) | WASM setup, minimal snippet, package boundaries |
| Enable Muse headset over Bluetooth (not a full walkthrough) | [Web Bluetooth With Supported Devices](/guides/web-bluetooth) | BLE transport on top of `eeg-web` |
| Understand federated learning plans | [Federated Learning Roadmap And Privacy](/guides/federated-learning) | Explains roadmap direction and privacy posture |

## Published Packages

| Package | Use it for | One-line summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `@elata-biosciences/create-elata-demo` | scaffolding demo apps | fastest way to get a working integration |
| `@elata-biosciences/rppg-web` | browser rPPG processing | camera-driven rPPG with `createRppgSession()` |
| `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` | browser EEG WASM APIs | EEG processing + model exports (add `eeg-web-ble` for live headset) |
| `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble` | Web Bluetooth for headsets | enables Muse-compatible streaming alongside `eeg-web` |

## Use The Right Workflow

The repo has a few internal flows that are helpful for SDK maintainers but are
not the normal consumer onboarding path.

- Use `create-elata-demo` for new apps and evaluation.
- Use published packages for existing browser apps.
- Use `./run.sh sync-to` only when you are editing `packages/eeg-web` inside
  this monorepo and want to link it into another local app.
- Treat in-repo demos as SDK-development surfaces, not the default install path.

## Repo Surfaces

The source repo also contains:

- Rust crates for EEG, rPPG, protocol support, and FFI
- generated WASM packaging flows
- demo templates and reference apps
- release tooling, changesets, and publish verification

If you are working on the SDK itself rather than consuming it, jump to the
maintainer pages.
